title: gmelina arborea
intro: Gmelina arborea, also known as the Indian beech or gamhar, is a deciduous tree native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to help treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections. The health benefits of Gmelina arborea include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Gmelina arborea include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and ointments for topical application.
